description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from dark, vertically aligned wooden staves. The barrel is reinforced by four evenly spaced metal hoops, three near the top and one near the bottom, which help maintain its structural integrity. The wood surface appears weathered and textured, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements or frequent use. The top and bottom are flat and closed, with no visible openings or spouts, indicating it is currently sealed. There are no handles or decorative markings, giving it a utilitarian appearance. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as warehouses, alleys, or storage yards.
description: A dark, weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops and a sealed top, featuring a classic bulging cylindrical shape and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height, bulging sides, and all four metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the curvature of the staves and the alignment of the metal hoops.
description_view_2: Another side view with similar features, showing the wood grain and the even spacing of the hoops.
description_view_3: Back view, nearly identical to the front, with the same visible hoops and wood texture.
